Visual cues like **status badges** (Critical, Warning, Healthy) and a **Sankey diagram** in the monitoring dashboard highlight automation health issues ([CrewAI Documentation](https://docs.crewai.com/en/enterprise/features/agent-control-plane/monitoring)).
ui_patterns: Node-and-edge visual canvas for workflow mapping
Execution vs. Visual Editor toggle view
Artifact gallery for generated file management
visual_description: The UI consists of a central **Visual Canvas** where agents and tasks are represented as connected nodes (nodes and edges). It is flanked by three main panels: **AI Thoughts (left)** showing streaming reasoning during design, **Canvas (center)** for the workflow graph, and **Resources (right)** for dragging-and-dropping agents, tasks, and tools ([CrewAI Documentation](https://docs.crewai.com/en/enterprise/features/crew-studio)). The 'Execution View' features an **Event Timeline**, detailed logs (Messages, Raw Data), and high-level execution metrics (Total Tokens, Estimated Cost) at the top ([CrewAI Documentation](https://docs.crewai.com/en/enterprise/features/traces)).

audience: Primarily targeted at developers for rapid prototyping, but also accessible to prosumers and researchers as a low-code/no-code interface ([Microsoft Research](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/blog/introducing-autogen-studio-a-low-code-interface-for-building-multi-agent-workflows/), [GitHub](https://github.com/microsoft/autogen)).
primary_surface: Web application (built with FastAPI and React) ([GitHub](https://github.com/microsoft/autogen/tree/main/python/packages/autogen-studio)).
task_lifecycle: Visualized through live message streaming, a "control transition graph" showing speaker transitions, and a session-based chat history. It also includes an "inner monologue" debug console for monitoring agent reasoning ([AutoGen Docs](https://microsoft.github.io/autogen/dev/user-guide/autogenstudio-user-guide/index.html), [YouTube: Introduction to AutoGen Studio v0.4](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oum6EI7wohM)).
approval_model: Human-in-the-loop is managed via the `UserProxyAgent` and `human_input_mode` settings (ALWAYS, TERMINATE, NEVER). In "ALWAYS" mode, the UI pauses execution and prompts the user for feedback or approval before proceeding. New v0.4 features include "Run Control," allowing users to manually pause or stop execution mid-run ([Microsoft Research](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/blog/introducing-autogen-studio-a-low-code-interface-for-building-multi-agent-workflows/), [AutoGen Docs](https://microsoft.github.io/autogen/dev/user-guide/autogenstudio-user-guide/usage.html)).
artifacts: Artifacts such as generated images, code blocks, text files, and performance metrics (token usage, cost) are surfaced directly within the Playground chat sessions. Users can also publish successful sessions to a "Gallery" for reference and reuse ([Microsoft Research Blog](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/blog/introducing-autogen-studio-a-low-code-interface-for-building-multi-agent-workflows/), [AutoGen Docs](https://microsoft.github.io/autogen/dev/user-guide/autogenstudio-user-guide/usage.html)).
multi_agent: Managed through independent "Sessions" (UUID-based threads) in the Playground. Users can spin up multiple sessions to test different teams or tasks in parallel ([YouTube: AutoGen Studio on Kubernetes](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-zwvvgORcp0), [AutoGen Usage](https://microsoft.github.io/autogen/dev/user-guide/autogenstudio-user-guide/usage.html)).
notifications: Urgent items bubble up via real-time streaming updates in the chat interface. "Approval Required" states intercept agent control and present interactive feedback prompts to the user within the active session ([Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/fr-fr/agent-framework/integrations/ag-ui/human-in-the-loop), [AutoGen Docs](https://microsoft.github.io/autogen/dev/user-guide/autogenstudio-user-guide/usage.html)).
ui_patterns: Component library/gallery for agents and skills
visual_description: The UI features a modern dark/light mode interface with a primary sidebar for navigating between Build, Playground, and Gallery. The Build section uses a node-based canvas for agent team composition, while the Playground resembles a standard AI chat interface with a sidebar for session history and a main window for live message streaming and artifact viewing ([AutoGen Usage Screenshots](https://microsoft.github.io/autogen/dev/user-guide/autogenstudio-user-guide/usage.html)).
sources: Microsoft Research Blog: Introducing AutoGen Studio

audience: Developer / Researcher
primary_surface: Web App (React/Flask) and CLI [Princeton NLP](https://princeton-nlp.github.io/SWE-agent/usage/web_ui/).
task_lifecycle: Visualized through a 'Trajectory Viewer' that displays the agent's step-by-step history (Thought, Action, Observation) in a chronological timeline [CSDN](https://blog.csdn.net/gitblog_00818/article/details/151412736).
approval_model: Largely autonomous (loops until submission or fatal error), with "Interactive Mode" enabling interactive tools like gdb. Quality Gates (linter integration) act as hard pre-action checkpoints that block syntactically incorrect edits before application [CSDN](https://blog.csdn.net/gitblog_00818/article/details/151412736), [Princeton NLP Docs](https://princeton-nlp.github.io/SWE-agent/usage/web_ui/).
artifacts: Artifacts are surfaced via a specialized 100-line windowed file viewer, a dedicated image viewer for multimodal screenshots, and final code diffs. Trajectories are stored as `.traj` files and visualized in the Inspector [Princeton NLP](https://princeton-nlp.github.io/SWE-agent/usage/inspector/), [arXiv](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2405.15793.pdf).
multi_agent: Batch mode allows running instances in parallel; the Trajectory Inspector provides a dashboard for browsing, filtering, and comparing results across hundreds of runs [Princeton NLP](https://princeton-nlp.github.io/SWE-agent/usage/batch_mode/).
notifications: Status is primarily surfaced through live log streaming and visual evaluation badges (✅/❌/❓) in the inspector dashboard [Princeton NLP Docs](https://swe-agent.com/latest/usage/inspector/).
visual_description: The UI features a multi-column layout. The top section contains configuration parameters (Issue URL, model selection). The bottom-left pane displays the agent's 'Thought' process, while the top-right pane shows the terminal environment (commands and output). A dedicated 'Logs' area is at the bottom right. The Trajectory Viewer includes a file sidebar for navigating multiple runs [CSDN](https://blog.csdn.net/gitblog_00818/article/details/151412736), [Issue #213](https://github.com/princeton-nlp/SWE-agent/issues/213).

audience: Developer and Prosumer (low-code) [DataCamp](https://www.datacamp.com/tutorial/autogpt-guide), [GitHub](https://github.com/significant-gravitas/autogpt).
primary_surface: Web Application (Next.js/React frontend) with a supporting CLI for developer setup [GitHub](https://github.com/significant-gravitas/autogpt), [Pyshine](https://pyshine.com/2026/04/20/autogpt-platform-continuous-ai-agents/).
task_lifecycle: Visualized through a **step-by-step reasoning log** (Copilot view) and a **real-time execution graph** where active blocks pulse or change color based on their state (e.g., green for success, red for error) [DataCamp](https://www.datacamp.com/tutorial/autogpt-guide), [Medium](https://aiedge.medium.com/autogpt-forge-interacting-with-your-agent-1214561b06b). Historical runs are tracked in a **"Tasks" tab** that displays a timeline of past executions and their outcomes [AutoGPT Docs](https://agpt.co/docs/platform/using-the-platform/agent-library).
approval_model: The platform supports **continuous mode** for full autonomy, but defaults to a **human-in-the-loop** model where the agent pauses for input at "Input Blocks" or when calling the "ask" command for manual confirmation [AutoGPT Docs](https://agpt.co/docs/platform/tutorials/create-basic-agent), [Lablab.ai](https://lablab.ai/ai-tutorials/auto-gpt-forge-tutorial). Users can also run agents step-by-step by manually approving each thought/action with a "y" input [Medium](https://aiedge.medium.com/autogpt-forge-interacting-with-your-agent-1214561b06b).
artifacts: Artifacts such as generated files, code, and images are surfaced as **rich cards** or **inline previews** within the chat-like interaction pane [DataCamp](https://www.datacamp.com/tutorial/autogpt-guide). A dedicated **"Agent Outputs"** pane consolidates structured results from multiple blocks, and users can click "View More" to see full execution logs and file outputs [AutoGPT Docs](https://agpt.co/docs/platform/using-the-platform/agent-library).
multi_agent: Multiple agents can be orchestrated as a **multi-agent system** by importing an existing agent as a "block" into a new workflow [AutoGPT Docs](https://agpt.co/docs/platform/agent-blocks). The **Monitoring Dashboard** provides a bird's-eye view of active executions, latency, and resource consumption across all running agents [Mutable.ai](https://mutable.ai/Significant-Gravitas/AutoGPT), [Pyshine](https://pyshine.com/2026/04/20/autogpt-platform-continuous-ai-agents/).
notifications: Urgent items and status updates bubble up via **in-app notification badges** (bell icon) and real-time status icons (green checkmarks for success, rotating circles for in-progress) [Medium](https://aiedge.medium.com/autogpt-forge-interacting-with-your-agent-1214561b06b). The platform also supports **email notifications** for failed tasks or run completions, which can be configured within the agent's settings [AutoGPT Releases](https://github.com/Significant-Gravitas/AutoGPT/releases).
ui_patterns: Low-code block-based workflow editor (Directed Acyclic Graph)
Agent as a Block (nesting agents within other workflows)
Marketplace-driven discovery with cards and ratings
Agent Library for personal agent management
Real-time monitoring dashboard with execution metrics
visual_description: The UI is a canvas-based **graph editor** with a large workspace for dragging and dropping "blocks" (nodes) connected by "wires" (data flows) [DataCamp](https://www.datacamp.com/tutorial/autogpt-guide). It features a dark-themed sidebar with icons for **Tasks, Library, Marketplace, and Settings** [AutoGPT Docs](https://agpt.co/docs/platform/using-the-platform/agent-library). The execution view resembles a chat interface (Copilot) where reasoning traces and outputs appear in a chronological feed, while property panels on the right allow for granular block configuration [Medium](https://aiedge.medium.com/autogpt-forge-interacting-with-your-agent-1214561b06b).

vendor: Agentic AI Foundation (AAIF) / Linux Foundation (originally Block / Square)
audience: Developer / Prosumer ([Andrew.ooo](https://andrew.ooo/posts/goose-review-open-source-ai-agent-block/), [Goose Docs](https://goose-docs.ai/docs/quickstart))
primary_surface: Native Desktop App (macOS, Linux, Windows), CLI, and API. It also functions as an **ACP (Agent Client Protocol) server** for IDEs like VS Code, Zed, and JetBrains ([GitHub](https://github.com/aaif-goose/goose), [Goose Docs](https://goose-docs.ai/docs/guides/acp-clients)).
task_lifecycle: The task lifecycle is visualized through a **step-by-step chat history** in the Desktop app and a **real-time dashboard** in the CLI for parallel runs, which shows task IDs, parameter sets, execution timing, and output previews ([Goose Docs](https://goose-docs.ai/docs/tutorials/subrecipes-in-parallel/#real-time-progress-monitoring)). Users can monitor progress through statistics of completed, running, failed, and pending tasks.
approval_model: Goose uses a **permission-based model** where users configure fine-grained tool permissions and allowlists ([Goose Docs](https://goose-docs.ai/docs/guides/managing-tools/tool-permissions)). It features an **Adversary Mode**, an independent reviewer agent that monitors tool calls for rogue behavior ([Goose Docs](https://goose-docs.ai/docs/guides/security/adversary-mode)), and **Prompt Injection Detection** to block harmful commands before execution ([Goose Docs](https://goose-docs.ai/docs/guides/security/prompt-injection-detection)).
artifacts: Artifacts like code diffs, generated files, and screenshots (via the screen toolkit) are surfaced directly within the **interactive chat** ([Goose Docs](https://goose-docs.ai/docs/guides/interactive-chat/)). It supports **MCP-UI and MCP Apps**, allowing agents to render rich graphical components like charts or custom interactive UIs inside the session ([Goose Blog](https://goose-docs.ai/blog/2026/01/06/mcp-apps)).
multi_agent: Goose manages multiple runs through **named sessions** stored in a local SQLite database ([Goose Docs](https://goose-docs.ai/docs/guides/logs/#session-records)). It can spawn **parallel subagents** and run **subrecipes in parallel** using isolated worker processes (up to 10 concurrently), monitored via a real-time CLI dashboard ([Goose Docs](https://goose-docs.ai/docs/tutorials/subrecipes-in-parallel)).
notifications: Urgent security findings (labeled `SEC-{uuid}`) bubble up as **in-app alerts** requiring explicit allow/deny decisions from the user ([Goose Docs](https://goose-docs.ai/docs/guides/logs/#system-logs)). Real-time progress stats (completed/running/failed) are shown during parallel batch runs in the CLI ([Goose Docs](https://goose-docs.ai/docs/tutorials/subrecipes-in-parallel/#real-time-progress-monitoring)).
ui_patterns: Recipe Cookbook for reusable YAML workflows
MCP-UI for rendering interactive graphical apps in-chat
Real-time parallel execution dashboard in CLI
visual_description: The Desktop UI features a clean, modern aesthetic with a **customizable sidebar** that can be positioned on any edge (default left) and toggled between Tile (large icons) or List styles ([Goose Docs](https://goose-docs.ai/docs/guides/desktop-navigation)). The main surface is a chat-centric interface that supports **dark mode** and is capable of rendering rich, non-textual **MCP-UI components** like seat selectors or data visualizations directly in the message flow ([Goose Docs](https://goose-docs.ai/docs/guides/interactive-chat/mcp-ui)).
